page: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 131 GB drive, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. The virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, scoring 2693.59 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25 percent of tested instances. However, due to the low core count, multi-core performance is weak, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent and bottom 10 percent respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 33.33 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.24 GB/sec. Database and web serving metrics are also low, with static web serving throughput at 2.57 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 2.06 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8-2as_v4 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-core processing capability despite the large memory footprint. This server is best suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads such as small-scale caching layers, lightweight in-memory databases, or specialized analytical tasks requiring a large memory space but minimal parallel CPU execution.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering a high resource density of 32.0 GB per core, alongside 131 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak due to the low core count. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-concurrency applications. It is best suited for lightweight in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads that do not require extensive parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 2 vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel compute needs.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-bound, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
Features 64.0 GB of RAM with a high 32.0 GB per core ratio
Equipped with 131 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads and caching
